title: Historical Simultaneous Worker Trigger Framework And Dormancy Planning
historical_note: This packet records one bounded Olares Phase 5 trigger-framework and dormancy planning slice from before the canonical repo boundary moved to `C:/APEX Platform/apex-power-ops-platform` on 2026-05-07. It remains packet-history provenance, not a live execution instruction for current repo operations.
current_routing: use PROJECT_STATUS.md for the current residue-retirement lane and latest completed packets
use docs/architecture/OLARES-PUBLICATION-BOUNDARY-RETIREMENT-DEPENDENCY-INVENTORY-2026-05-06.md for the remaining post-cutover boundary closeout queue
use this packet JSON only when historical provenance is needed for the earlier `olares-phase-5-092` trigger-framework and dormancy planning slice
objective: Define the planning-only trigger framework and dormancy posture for reopening later paired apparatus/relay objective selection after Packet 090 found no current real paired objective set.
admissible_future_trigger_sources: A newly reported bounded defect in apparatus-owned and relay-owned surfaces, with concrete evidence for both sides and no coordinator-owned file requirement.
A newly failing owned apparatus or relay smoke test that independently identifies bounded work inside the published worker-owned files.
A later packetized one-worker outcome that creates a real paired follow-on objective on both apparatus-owned and relay-owned surfaces without shared-file overlap.

A newly authored bounded requirement that independently lands inside apparatus-owned and relay-owned files and does not require static-surfaces validation, app-wide layout/style/config, package files, lockfiles, runtime, service, install, or old-clone mutation.
A documented call-site mismatch inside the published worker-owned source/helper surfaces that can be cited by path and does not require mutating shared fixtures or coordinator-owned files.
inadmissible_future_trigger_sources: Re-running the Packet 090 read-only discovery over the same owned surfaces with no new repo evidence.
Selecting work whose purpose is only to exercise simultaneous-worker machinery.
Cosmetic churn, naming churn, or test assertion rearrangement without a repository-evidenced defect or bounded requirement.

Any objective requiring mutation of shared `browser-env`, static-surfaces validation, app-wide layout, global style, Playwright config, Next config, package files, lockfiles, installs, runtime, services, remotes, rollback/force/reset/clean, or old clone.
Any objective requiring a change to Packet 082 ownership or Packet 084 abort rules without a separate planning packet.
Any objective where both workers need the same file or where one worker needs another worker-owned file.
reopen_criteria_for_later_paired_objecti: The future packet must cite new evidence created or discovered after Packet 090.
The evidence must identify one bounded apparatus objective and one bounded relay objective.
Each objective must map entirely to the published Packet 082 worker-owned files.
The future packet must prove no coordinator-owned file, package/lockfile, runtime/service, install, remote, rollback, force/reset/clean, old-clone, or shared-fixture mutation is needed.
The future packet must restate the exact changed-file ownership and abort conditions before any execution-opening decision.
The future packet must select paired objectives before any explicit simultaneous-worker execution packet can open.
evidence_floor: Exact file paths and line-level or test-level evidence for the apparatus side.
Exact file paths and line-level or test-level evidence for the relay side.
A statement explaining why the pair is real work rather than synthetic machinery exercise.
A non-overlap proof against Packet 082 ownership and Packet 084 abort rules.
A validation plan that includes coordinator-owned static-surfaces validation without assigning it to either worker.
next_packet_candidate: Olares Phase 5 093 - Packet 091 And Packet 092 Authority Publication And Host Mirror Resync Gate
sql_permission_note: This planning packet authorizes no database writes and no source/test execution, simultaneous multi-worker mutation, paired-objective selection, migration approval, runtime mutation, service mutation, package mutation, lockfile mutation, install, package-manager activation/download, remote rewrite, AI-services expansion, Gitea/code-hosting transition, canonical-hosting transition, rollback, force/reset/clean, or old-clone mutation.
